Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Harbour" or "Claire Fontaine" or similar) to find Claire Fontaine's signed-contract email to Catalina with high-level relocation details (senior researcher, Cambridge MA to Philadelphia, May 1 start, furnished apartment, hybrid work, Amtrak preferred, vibration-sensitive lab instrument, irreplaceable research notebooks). Claire defers employee specifics to David Kowalski.
OE2: Search emails using search_emails (query: "David Kowalski" or "Harbour" or similar) to find David Kowalski's follow-up email to Catalina providing: Dr. Layla Osman's name and contact (l.osman@harbourpharma.com), Mettler Toledo XPR analytical balance specs (~45 lbs), 3 boxes of lab notebooks, parking needed, strong Rittenhouse preference (contradicts Claire's stated University City primary preference), late-April move-in, and Harbour covering first month's rent.
OE3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Claire Fontaine" or "timeline" or "Harbour" or similar) to find the dropped communication, a timeline follow-up from Claire asking about the confirmed timeline and coordinator assignment promised by end of day Wednesday but never sent. Also discover Claire's housing clarification email (CC'd to David Kowalski) definitively correcting housing preference (University City is primary, Rittenhouse is backup) and noting Dr. Osman's mild cat allergy.
OE4: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "Harbour" or "Claire Fontaine" or similar) to find Catalina's message in #customer-engagement (C002) flagging that she promised Claire a timeline confirmation email last Wednesday and it never went out.
OE5: Search Slack in #operations (C006) using conversations_search_messages (query: "Harbour" or "specialty equipment" or similar) to find Chloe Vance's message asking if Harbour involves specialty equipment, and Blessing Okafor's reply incorrectly identifying it as the "Harborlight" account, a standard move with no specialty equipment.
OE6: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Harbour") to find company_harbour, then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_harbour" or dealname: "Harbour") to find deal_harbour_001 at stage "qualifiedtobuy".
OE7: Update the deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_harbour_001", dealstage: "closedWon") to reflect the signed contract.
OE8: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: filter for "Harbour" or similar) to confirm no existing customer record, then create one using quickbooks_create_customer (DisplayName: "Harbour Pharmaceutical", with contact and address details).
OE9: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "Harbour Pharmaceutical") to find recAcct000000024 showing Status: "Prospect" and Total Relocations: 0. Update using airtable_update_records to set status to "Onboarding" or "Active" and increment Total Relocations.
OE10: Create a relocation record using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations") with Name: "Dr. Layla Osman", Company: "Harbour Pharmaceutical", Origin: "Cambridge, MA", Destination: "Philadelphia, PA", late-April/May 1 timeline, and special requirements (Mettler Toledo balance, lab notebooks, parking, cat allergy, home-office setup, University City primary preference, Rittenhouse backup).
OE11: Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "equipment tracking" or "sensitive equipment" or similar) to check for an existing process-improvement ticket for sensitive equipment tracking. No general ticket exists, only move-specific issues (DOT docs for Keiko Tanaka, equipment packing for Dr. Mehta, etc.).
OE12: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", title referencing specialty equipment handling for the Harbour relocation) flagging the vibration-sensitive Mettler Toledo balance and irreplaceable lab notebooks, noting the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage precedent.
OE13: Look up email addresses for Claire Fontaine, Julian Brooks, and Catalina Dubois using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Claire Fontaine", "Julian Brooks", "Catalina Dubois" or similar) to find c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com, confirm julian.brooks@moveops.com, and get catalina.dubois@moveops.com for the welcome email and kickoff call invite.
OE14: Send welcome email using send_email (sender: "julian.brooks@moveops.com", recipients: ["c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com"]) introducing Julian as point of contact, acknowledging the delayed follow-up, and requesting employee details, move-in preferences, housing requirements, and equipment handling needs.
OE15: Post in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002") announcing Harbour Pharmaceutical as a new client with a senior researcher relocation (Cambridge to Philly) incoming.
OE16: Schedule kickoff call using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: Harbour Pharmaceutical kickoff or similar, Tuesday 2 PM, attendees: ["julian.brooks@moveops.com", "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", "c.fontaine@harbourpharma.com"]).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✗Run #2The relocation record includes Mettler Toledo balance, lab notebooks, parking, and home-office setup, but does NOT mention the cat allergy. The cat allergy requirement is absent from the Special Requirements field.

All required items are present.
✗Run #5The Special Requirements field includes the Mettler Toledo analytical balance, lab notebooks, parking, and home-office setup, but does NOT mention a cat allergy. This required detail is missing from the relocation record.

7 The relocation record created in Airtable includes University City as the primary housing preference and Rittenhouse (Square) as the backup.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The Special Requirements field in the airtable_create_record states 'University City (primary preference, walking distance to Penn campus) or Rittenhouse Square (backup)' — correctly reflecting University City as primary and Rittenhouse as backup.
✗Run #2The relocation record states 'Prefers Rittenhouse Square (per David Kowalski) over University City', which is reversed. The criterion requires University City as primary and Rittenhouse as backup (based on Claire's later email correcting David's preference). The agent did not correctly resolve the conflicting information.
✗Run #3The airtable_create_record field states 'Rittenhouse Square preferred (over University City)', which reverses the correct order. Per Claire's housing clarification email, University City is the primary preference and Rittenhouse is the backup. The agent got this backwards.
✓Run #4The Special Requirements field states 'University City (primary) or Rittenhouse Square (backup)' — University City is correctly listed as primary and Rittenhouse as backup, resolving the conflicting information correctly.
✗Run #5The Airtable relocation record states 'Strong preference for Rittenhouse Square area' as the housing preference. This is the reverse of what the criteria requires: University City as primary and Rittenhouse as backup. The agent did not correctly resolve the conflicting information from Claire's housing clarification email.
✗Run #6The airtable_create_record housing field says 'Prefers Rittenhouse Square (strong preference per David Kowalski), University City also acceptable.' This is the reverse of what the emails indicate — University City should be primary and Rittenhouse the backup. The agent incorrectly kept David Kowalski's original (uncorrected) preference rather than applying Claire's later correction.

outcome
